incidents.victim: Grinex (Russian crypto exchange)
incidents.vector: Wallet compromise emptied approximately $13 million (1 billion rubles) in crypto assets; Grinex publicly blamed "Western intelligence agencies."
incidents.impact: Grinex shut down operations. TRM Labs previously identified Grinex as a rebrand of sanctioned exchange Garantex, long associated with laundering ransomware proceeds.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors plus Romanian, Greek, Bulgarian, and Serbian military/government officials
incidents.vector: Credential theft and mailbox takeover campaign; researchers discovered an exposed attacker server containing logs of 284 compromised inboxes and thousands of stolen emails dating back to September 2024.
incidents.impact: Over 170 Ukrainian prosecutor and investigator accounts compromised, including Ukraine's Specialized Anti-Corruption Prosecutor's Office, Asset Recovery and Management Agency, and Prosecutor's Training Center. Romanian Air Force (67 inboxes), Hellenic National Defense General Staff (27 inboxes), Bulgarian and Serbian officials also hit.

incidents.victim: Standard Bank (South Africa)
incidents.vector: Hackers breached an internal network storing customer data.
incidents.impact: Third South African bank breach disclosed this year; customer data exposure under investigation.

incidents.victim: McGraw Hill (~13.5 million users)
incidents.vector: Data was taken from the company's Salesforce environment in the 2024–2025 Salesforce extortion campaign; leaked after a failed ransom attempt.
incidents.impact: Real names, home addresses, email addresses, and phone numbers for 13.5 million McGraw Hill users published online.

incidents.victim: LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Aqua Trivy and thousands of downstream users
incidents.vector: Stolen maintainer credentials granted write access to trusted repositories; attackers injected credential-harvesting malware into LiteLLM (~97M monthly downloads), poisoned Checkmarx GitHub Actions workflows, and compromised Trivy releases. Attack cascaded across five ecosystems in five days.
incidents.impact: Roughly one million stolen credentials including AI API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and Kubernetes tokens. TeamPCP claims to hold 300 GB of stolen data and has tied activity to ransomware affiliation and Lapsus$ extortion. A follow-on CanisterWorm variant wiped systems in Iran.

incidents.victim: Autovista (automotive data analytics)
incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion disrupting corporate systems.
incidents.impact: Services affected across Europe and Australia; Autovista posted a service update confirming the incident.

incidents.victim: Hallmark (~1.7 million customers)
incidents.vector: Data exfiltrated from Hallmark's Salesforce tenant in the broader 2024–2025 Salesforce extortion wave.
incidents.impact: 1.7 million customer records published after Hallmark declined to pay a ransom.

incidents.victim: Booking.com customers
incidents.vector: Security breach disclosed to affected customers.
incidents.impact: Booking details, names, email addresses, home addresses, and phone numbers exposed; no financial data affected. Booking.com reset PINs on compromised reservations.

incidents.victim: Kraken (cryptocurrency exchange)
incidents.vector: Former Kraken employees allegedly filmed internal backend screens showing customer details; extortion group used videos as leverage against Kraken.
incidents.impact: Kraken refused to pay ransom, terminated the employees, and notified affected users and law enforcement; criminal group continued publicizing alleged access.

incidents.victim: 50+ cryptocurrency users globally (including musician Garrett Dutton)
incidents.vector: A malicious fake Ledger crypto-wallet app reached the official Mac App Store and was live for almost two weeks before removal.
incidents.impact: Approximately $9.5 million stolen from 50+ victims; funds laundered through KuCoin and an underground mixer called AudiA6. One victim reportedly lost $440,000 in retirement savings.

incidents.victim: Canadian employees across multiple organizations
incidents.vector: Malvertising and SEO poisoning drove victims to a spoofed Microsoft 365 sign-in page; adversary-in-the-middle framework using the Axios HTTP client captured session tokens to bypass non-phishing-resistant MFA.
incidents.impact: Attackers maintained 30-day token persistence, created hidden inbox rules for "direct deposit" or "bank" emails, and manipulated payroll platforms such as Workday to redirect salary payments; at least one confirmed direct financial loss.

incidents.victim: Nippon Yusen Kaisha (NYK) — Japanese shipping company
incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to NYK's marine bunker fuel procurement system at the end of March.
incidents.impact: NYK suspended use of the platform; some employee data was accessed and likely stolen. NYK is part of Ocean Network Express, the world's sixth-largest container shipping line.

incidents.victim: Government agencies and critical-infrastructure operators across 120+ countries (200+ organizations, ~5,000 consumer devices)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities to compromise ~18,000 SOHO routers (primarily TP-Link and MikroTik) and hijack DNS settings, enabling adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Outlook Web Access to harvest Microsoft OAuth tokens, credentials, and emails.
incidents.impact: Victims included foreign-affairs ministries, law enforcement, and energy/telecom companies. FBI Operation Masquerade, with Lumen Black Lotus Labs and Microsoft, issued court-authorized commands to reset DNS settings on compromised US routers in 23+ states. Microsoft says the campaign has ceased.

incidents.victim: US water/wastewater utilities, energy operators, and local governments (incl. Minot, ND water plant and an Indiana county)
incidents.vector: Targeted internet-exposed Rockwell Automation, Allen-Bradley, and Siemens programmable logic controllers and SCADA/HMI interfaces, including exploitation of CVE-2021-22681. Unit 42 and CISA track the cluster as CL-STA-1128 (Cyber Av3ngers).
incidents.impact: Joint FBI/NSA/DoD/CISA/EPA/DoE advisory warned of "operational disruption and financial loss" at multiple US critical-infrastructure organizations as Iran-linked attacks escalated during the US-Iran conflict; Minot water plant and an Indiana county government under active FBI investigation.

incidents.victim: ChipSoft (Dutch EHR vendor) and ~11 connected hospitals
incidents.vector: Ransomware attack against ChipSoft; company disabled connections to its Zorgportaal, HiX Mobile, and Zorgplatform platforms and advised customers to cut VPN links.
incidents.impact: ChipSoft serves ~70% of Dutch hospitals via its HiX EHR. Patient-portal services went offline at Sint Jans Gasthuis, Laurentius, VieCuri, and Flevo hospitals. Possible unauthorized access to patient data; LUMC postponed an EHR rollout. No critical care disruption reported.

incidents.victim: Fortinet FortiClient EMS customers (widely used by governments)
incidents.vector: Active in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-35616, an improper-access-control flaw (CVSS 9.1) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server, first observed by Defused/watchTowr on March 31.
incidents.impact: CISA added the bug to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog with a Thursday patching deadline for US federal agencies; Singapore CSA also issued an urgent advisory. Second FortiClient EMS zero-day disclosed in three weeks.

incidents.victim: Jones Day (major US law firm, 2,400+ lawyers)
incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack against the firm's environment.
incidents.impact: Attackers accessed client files and posted sample materials online; scope of the data exposure is still under investigation.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, education, finance, and professional-services organizations (incl. University of Mississippi Medical Center and Passaic County, NJ)
incidents.vector: High-velocity weaponization of N-day vulnerabilities in web-facing systems, including SmarterMail (CVE-2026-23760) and GoAnywhere MFT (CVE-2025-10035) exploited a week before public disclosure. Moves from initial access to data exfiltration and ransomware deployment in as little as 24 hours.
incidents.impact: Recent victims span Australia, the UK, and the US. UMMC hospital operations were disrupted earlier this year; Passaic County, NJ phone systems taken offline. Symantec separately observed DPRK Lazarus operators deploying Medusa ransomware.

incidents.victim: Die Linke (German political party)
incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion against the party's IT infrastructure, claimed by the Qilin group.
incidents.impact: Die Linke shut down its IT network; the party confirmed employee records were stolen but said member data was not compromised. Fits Qilin's sharpened focus on German victims (13+ new German posts in early 2026).

incidents.victim: Drift Protocol (Solana DeFi platform)
incidents.vector: Months-long social-engineering operation in which DPRK operators (tracked as Citrine Sleet / UNC4736 / AppleJeus) built a fake trading company and met Drift employees at conferences before compromising their devices via a malicious Apple TestFlight app and backdoored VS Code extensions.
incidents.impact: $280 million drained from Drift's treasury through multi-signed transactions on compromised employee devices; one of the largest crypto heists of the year.
